response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but his technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary stack throughout his career has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with Java appearing only marginally in his skills list and briefly in one role (robotic arms tooling). There is no demonstrated experience with Kotlin, the Spring ecosystem, Hibernate, Quarkus, JVM internals, or enterprise application server integration — all of which are core requirements. While he has some IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI) and developer tooling experience, these are in C/C++ and TypeScript respectively, not JVM-based tooling.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and IDE internals through work at National Instruments (C IDE) and MCP/agentic tooling at re:cinq
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) applicable to any engineering context
Built and maintained open-source language libraries and test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), showing genuine interest in language tooling
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience; Java appears in skills but is only referenced in a minor robotic-arm context — no JVM depth demonstrated
Zero Spring ecosystem experience (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate not mentioned anywhere in resume)
Primary career trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js web and GIS development, which is orthogonal to enterprise JVM IDE tooling
